title: Effects of Spirulina Supplementation on Mental Function in Healthy Adults: a Randomized Controlled Trial
authors_full: Presenting: Carol S. Johnston; Co-Authors: Kelsey Clay (NTR MS candidate) and Hannah Wheeling (NTR Barrett Honors student)
authors_short: Presenting Carol S. Johnston et al.

summary.hook: This randomized controlled trial tested whether spirulina supplementation (3 g/day for 8 weeks) improves mood and cognitive performance in healthy adults compared with placebo.
summary.summary_30s: This randomized controlled trial tested whether spirulina supplementation (3 g/day for 8 weeks) improves mood and cognitive performance in healthy adults compared with placebo. Mood was assessed using the POMS questionnaire (six subscales), and cognition using the Trail Making Test (TMT-A, TMT-B, and TMT B–A).

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This randomized controlled trial tested whether spirulina supplementation (3 g/day for 8 weeks) improves mood and cognitive performance in healthy adults compared with...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Randomized controlled trial (supplement vs placebo).. Not stated on poster
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Total POMS score: Spirulina group shows a trend toward improvement from baseline to week 8 (pre/post paired t-test p=0.074).
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Spirulina may have small beneficial effects on mood domains and aspects of executive functioning in healthy adults over 8 weeks.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: How many participants were randomized to SPIR vs CON, and what were baseline characteristics (age, sex, BMI, diet patterns)?

key_numbers: Total POMS pre/post in spirulina group: p=0.074 (paired t-test).
Between-group interactions not significant (repeated-measures ANOVA; p>0.05).
takeaways: Total POMS score: Spirulina group shows a trend toward improvement from baseline to week 8 (pre/post paired t-test p=0.074).
POMS subscales (tension/anger/fatigue etc.) show asterisked significant time effects in some panels (exact p-values not printed).
TMT outcomes: Asterisks denote significant pre/post time effects; however between-group interactions not significant (repeated-measures ANOVA p>0.05).
